name: Roast Duckling
recipeIngredient: 1 carrot, washed, scraped, and coarsely chopped
3 to 4 tbsp lard, duck fat, or chicken fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F to 325°F.
2. Use a roasting pan with a tight-fitting cover. Add lard, duck fat, or chicken fat into the roasting pan.
3. Remove the neck and giblets from the duckling cavity. Rub the inside and outside of the duckling with Chef's Salt.
4. Place the duckling, breast down, directly on top of the lump of fat in the roasting pan. Add cut vegetables, garlic, water (1 to 2 inches), peppercorns, bay leaf, and marjoram. Cover and place in the preheated oven.
5. After 2 hours, carefully remove the duckling from the roasting pan to a platter. Allow it to cool completely.
6. Split the duckling lengthwise by standing it on the neck end and cutting from the tail tip down the center. Quarter each half if desired.
7. Place the cold, split duckling pieces cut side down and skin side up on a slightly greased cookie sheet. Return to an oven set to 425°F to 450°F for 18 to 22 minutes. Before serving, remove the first two wing joints.
Chef's Secret: Buying a high-quality duckling (4 1/2 to 5 lbs) is key for success. Frozen ducklings are safe and economical. Store them in the freezer until ready to use. Defrost overnight in the refrigerator. The duckling can be kept at room temperature or refrigerated if it becomes too hot. Bringing it back to room temperature before finishing cooking prevents a steamed taste.
The addition of fat to the duck creates an even surface over boiling water, preventing steam-cooked flavors and allowing the fat under the skin to release naturally.
